zh.lifeSummary: Jan de Meyer於1555年出生在荷蘭-拉藍德。她於1618年3月21日與Femmosijn Breyk在荷蘭-拉藍德萊登結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1618年在英國享年63歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Jan de Meyer于1555年出生在荷兰-拉藍德。她于1618年3月21日与Femmosijn Breyk在荷兰-拉藍德萊登结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1618年在英国享年63岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Meyer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German and Swiss German: from Middle High German meier, a status name for a steward, bailiff, or overseer, which later came to be used also to denote a tenant farmer, which is normally the sense in the many compound surnames formed with this term as a second element. Originally it denoted a village headman (ultimately from Latin maior ‘greater, superior’). This form of the surname is also established in France (mainly Alsace and Lorraine); see also 3 below. Compare Maier , Mayer , Meier , and Myer .

Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the Yiddish personal name Meyer, from Hebrew Meir ‘enlightener’, a derivative of or ‘light’ with the prefix m-. Compare Maier , Majer , Major , Mayer , Mayor , Meier , and Meir .
Dutch: variant, also Flemish and Americanized, of   Meijer  , a cognate of 1 above. This surname is also established in South Africa, where it was also brought from France (see 1 above).

variants.name: Majoros
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Attila, Janos, Katalin.

Hungarian: from a shortened form of majorosgazda, a status name for a senior tenant farmer or one who had been granted a particularly large land holding, Hungarian major (from Latin maior ‘greater’ or from German Meyer ).

variants.name: Maier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Otto, Helmut, Manfred, Gerhard, Lothar, Armin, Erwin, Franz, Heinz, Rudi.

German and Dutch (of German origin): variant of   Mayer   (see   Meyer   1). The German surname in this form is also found in some other European countries, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Czechia.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from a variant of the personal name Meyer (see Meyer 2).

variants.name: Lindenmeyer
meanings: German: distinguishing name for the tenant (see Meyer ) of a farm identified by a lime tree, Middle High German linde.

variants.name: Majer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Armin, Erwin, Georg, Gerhard, Manfred, Ulrich, Wolfgang.  Jewish Aron.

Polish, Czech, Slovak, Slovenian, Croatian, and Serbian; Hungarian (also Májer): status name for a steward, bailiff, or tenant farmer, from a Slavicized or Hungarized form of German Meyer 1, Mayer 4. As a Slovenian surname it is derived from the loanword majer ‘steward, bailiff’, which also denoted a hired worker on a forest estate. Compare Maier 4 and Mayer 6.
Slovak: from majer ‘manor farm estate’, a loanword from German (compare 1 above), probably used to denote a person living and working at the farm. Compare Majernik .

variants.name: Mayr
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Franz, Otto, Siegfried, Ernst, Hans, Helmut, Konrad, Kurt, Armin, Bernd, Dieter, Fritz.
German: variant of   Meyer   1.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): variant of   Meyer   2.

variants.name: Lenzmeier
meanings: German: distinguishing name for a tenant farmer (see   Meyer  ) with the personal name   Lenz  .

variants.name: Longmire
meanings: English: habitational name from a minor place in the parish of Windermere (Westmorland), named with Old Scandinavian langr ‘long’ + mýrr ‘mire, bog’.
Americanized form of German Langmeier or of any of its variants: distinguishing name for a tenant farmer (see Meyer 1) whose farm held the name Lang or who was of a tall stature.

variants.name: Lodermeier
meanings: North German: unflattering distinguishing nickname for a sloppy farmer, from Middle Low German loder ‘sloppy person or worker’ + meier ‘tenant farmer’ (see Meyer ).

variants.name: Luetkemeyer
meanings: North German (Lütkemeyer): distinguishing name either for a tenant farmer (see Meyer ) whose name was Lüdeke (see Luedecke ) or for one of diminutive stature (from Middle Low German luttik ‘small, little’).

variants.name: Linsenmeyer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Fritz, Monika, Otto.
German:
distinguishing name for a tenant farmer (see Meyer ) who grew lentils, from Middle High German lins(e) ‘lentil’.

nameMeanings.name: Jan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.
William of Orange was recognized as viceroy of Holland, Friesland and Utrecht.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.
Britain waged wars against the Dutch.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.description: Rembrandt Harmenszoon van Rijn was born in Leiden, the Netherlands.
"English navigator Henry Hudson, under contract to the United East India Company, sails from Amsterdam and ""discovers""Manhattan Island and the future site of New York."

en.lifeSummary: Jan de Meyer was born in 1555, in South Holland, Netherlands. She married Femmosijn Breyk on 21 March 1618, in Leiden, South Holland, Netherlands. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1618, in England, at the age of 63.

fr.lifeSummary: Jan de Meyer est née en 1555, à Hollande-Méridionale, Pays-Bas. Elle a épousé Femmosijn Breyk le 21 mars 1618, à Leiden, Hollande-Méridionale, Pays-Bas. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée en 1618, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 63 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Jan de Meyer wurde 1555 in Südholland, Niederlande geboren. Sie hat am 21. März 1618 in Leiden, Südholland, Niederlande Femmosijn Breyk geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb 1618 im Alter von 63 Jahren in England.

it.lifeSummary: Jan de Meyer è nata il nel 1555 nel Zuid-Holland, Paesi Bassi. Lei sposato Femmosijn Breyk il 21 marzo 1618, nel Leiden, Zuid-Holland, Paesi Bassi. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì nel 1618, nel England, all'età di 63 anni.

ja.South Holland, Netherlands: オランダズイド-hオランド
ja.Lincolnshire, England: イギリスリンコルンシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: Jan de Meyerは1555年にオランダズイド-hオランドで生まれた。1618年3月21日にオランダズイド-hオランドレイデンでFemmosijn Breykと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1618年にイギリスで63歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jan de Meyer(은)는 1555년 네덜란드 주이드-홀랜드에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1618년 3월 21일 네덜란드 주이드-홀랜드 레이든에서 Femmosijn Breyk(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1618년 영국에서 63세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Jan de Meyer nasceu em 1555, em Zuid-Holland, Países Baixos. Ela casou-se com Femmosijn Breyk em 21 de março de 1618, em Leiden, Zuid-Holland, Países Baixos. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ela faleceu em 1618, em Inglaterra, com 63 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Jan de Meyer родилась в 1555 г. в местности зуид-холланд, Нидерланды. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Femmosijn Breyk 21 марта 1618 г. в местности лиден, зуид-холланд, Нидерланды. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла в 1618 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 63 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Jan de Meyer nació en 1555, en Zuid-Holland, Países Bajos. Se casó con Femmosijn Breyk el 21 de marzo de 1618, en Leiden, Zuid-Holland, Países Bajos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió en 1618, en Inglaterra, a la edad de 63 años.
